content: Hey, I'm so glad it's Friday. I've been working non-stop from my home office in Phoenix all week.
I'm glad you're looking forward to the weekend! What do you have planned?
I'm actually planning to meet up with my sister, Maya, who's visiting from California. We're going to grab coffee at this new cafe that just opened up downtown.
That sounds like a great way to spend time with your sister. What's your favorite type of coffee?
I'm a big fan of pour-overs. I've even started experimenting with brewing my own coffee at home using a Chemex.
That's really cool! I've heard great things about the Chemex. Do you have any fun plans for the rest of the weekend with your sister?
We're thinking about checking out a tennis tournament on Saturday. I've been trying to get back into playing tennis regularly, but it's been tough with my work schedule.
It can be challenging to balance work and hobbies. How do you like working remotely from Phoenix compared to the Bay Area?
I really love it. The cost of living is so much lower here, and I can focus on my work as a UX researcher without all the distractions of the city.
That makes sense. Have you gotten involved in any local communities or groups since moving to Phoenix?
Yeah, I've joined a few groups for DJing and origami, which has been a great way to meet new people who share similar interests.
That's awesome! It's great that you're staying connected with your passions. Do you have any upcoming projects or events that you're excited about?
Actually, I'm planning to perform at a local music festival on April 15th, which I'm really looking forward to.
That sounds like a blast! I'm sure you'll do great. Have you thought about what you're going to play at the festival?
I'm thinking about playing a mix of electronic and hip-hop sets. I've been experimenting with some new beats and rhythms.
That sounds like a great setlist. I'm sure the crowd will love it. Do you have any other fun plans for the spring?
My sister and I are thinking about taking a trip to Sedona on April 22nd to do some hiking and explore the area.
Sedona is beautiful this time of year. You should definitely check out the red rocks and take in the views.
Definitely. I've heard great things about the hiking trails there. Okay, I should probably get going, but it was great chatting with you!
You too, Zion! Have a great weekend and good luck with your music festival performance!
